references.title: GritNet: Student performance prediction with deep learning

extracted.Students: 1853 students in ND-A and 8301 ND-B students
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
As a Deep Learning Model that uses a Bidirectional LSTM.

Tuning was done via GridSearch.
extracted.Comments:
Authors present their architecture called GritNet.

This embedding was compared against Logistic regressions.

annotations.text: The authors start from the perspective that there are very few attempts at using Deep Learning to address the student performance prediction problem. The authors, thus, propose a Bi-Directional LSTM based model that treats the problem of student performance prediction as a sequential event prediction problem.

While working with real Udacity data, authors show that GritNet outperforms Logistic Regression in the overall task of predicting student success. Moreover, the differences are more pronounced in the first few weeks of the course.
